subject: Biology
title: Photosynthesis and Climate
description: Learn Photosynthesis and Climate in one minute.

hook.text: Plants absorb about 120 billion tons of carbon from the atmosphere each year.

buildup.text: Forests, grasslands, and ocean algae are massive carbon sinks, slowing climate change.

discovery.text: Rising CO₂ levels initially boost plant growth—a temporary effect called CO₂ fertilization.

twist.text: But heat stress, drought, and nutrient limits cancel out the gains. Plants can't save us alone.

climax.text: Protecting and restoring forests is one of the most effective climate strategies available.

punchline.text: Plants are Earth's lungs. They need protection.

quiz.question: What is CO₂ fertilization?
options: Higher CO₂ temporarily boosting plant growth
